www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 11 --

Welcome and Course Outline
● Week 1 & 2 - Get Money Now
● Week 3 - Conversion Focused Copy
● Week 4 - System Automation
● Week 5 - Bringing Them In
● Week 6 - Building the Bond
● Week 7 - Scaling
● Week 8 - Diagnosis
www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 11 --

Setting Your Target
● SANDOR Model
● First thing that must be done is to set a realistic target
● Key Components
○ Real number amount
○ Real date target
● Once you have your target, you can begin the process of reverse engineering
your success. With MATH
www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 11 --

Getting Asset Information
● Two important pieces to this
● Breadth
○ Getting every possible different asset
● Depth
○ Getting data on each of these assets
www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 11 --

Getting Info
● Important to understand that business owners are almost always focused on
running their business, and not on working on growing their business
● They often are not going to know many of the questions you ask them
● You will have to prod and poke to get the info from them.
● This also applies to YOU
www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 11 --

Repeat what works
● Almost all of the time there is something that was done that worked really well,
or better than usual
● Almost all of the time, it was also never repeated
● Become very comfortable with your tech tools. Understand where to find this
hidden gold.
● This process is the core process that we use to make our clients money
quickly
www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 11 --

Click + Countdown Sequence
● This is a super simple process, but it WORKS
● This is the foundation for just about every campaign we run
● Click sequence is to get someone to indicate they are interested in getting
help by doing something
● Countdown sequence is used to push them to convert
● Taking action and getting stuff out is going to be a MUCH bigger factor in
performance vs. having the ‘perfect email sequence’ (it doesn’t exist)
www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 11 --

Deadline Stack
● Add bonuses to re-run your deadline
● Do not fall into the trap of thinking people don’t buy because they don’t want it
● They probably either didn’t see the email, or put it off, or just ignored it
● Roughly 80% of the time, a person is NOT open to making a purchase
● The idea is to have more coverage to hit them in the 20%
● Main angle for each consecutive stack is - this worked really well so I wanted to add
X
● Bonuses
● Payment Plans
● Trials
www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 11 --

Escape and Arrival
● This is the BIG DADDY of product creation
● Also helps when looking at your overall product/service offering
● Infinitely Expandable or Compressible
● Important to know where your clients ARE and where they want to BE
● Every step along the way is potential for a product
● You can combine steps (but probably not too many)
www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 11 --

Q&A
● Multiple Products, what to start with?
○ Some success with lower end products on Facebook
○ Find out how much money you lost per customer
○ How much does it cost to GET a customer
● People not opening emails
○ Software program similar to CF
○ Subject lines
○ Where are they coming from?
○ Congruency
www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 11 --
